questions.body: Vernier callipers have 20 divisions on its vernier scale which coincide with 19 divisions on the main scale. Least count of the instrument is 0.1 mm. The main scale division is:
questions.subject: physics
questions.solution:
Least count = value of 1 division of main scale/Total division of vernier scale
0.1 = x/20 => x = 2 mm
= (1 - (sin^2 A + cos^2 A - 2 sin A cos A))/cos A sin A
= (2 sin A cos A)/sin A cos A = 2.

questions.body: L, M and N are mid points of sides AB, BC and CA of triangle ABC. If area of triangle ABC is 48 sq. units, the area of triangle LMN will be:
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
LN = (1/2)BC [by mid-point theorem]
ΔABC ~ ΔMNL [By AA similarity]
Area ΔABC/Area ΔMNL = (BC/LN)^2 = (2)^2 = 4
area ΔLMN = area ΔABC/4 = 48/4 = 12 sq.unit.

questions.body: The expression (1 - tan A + sec A) (1 - cot A + cosec A) has value:
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
(1 - tan A + sec A) (1 - cot A + cosec A)
= (1 - sin A/cos A + 1/cos A)(1 - cos A/sin A + 1/sin A)
= (cos A - sin A + 1)/cos A × (sin A - cos A + 1)/sin A
= (1 - (sin A - cos A))(1 + (sin A - cos A))/cos A sin A
= (1^2 - (sin A - cos A)^2)/cos A sin A

questions.body: A certain force acting on a body of mass 2 kg increases its velocity from 6 m/s to 15 m/s in 2 s. The work done by the force during this interval is:
questions.subject: physics
questions.solution:
Work done = Change in kinetic energy =
(1/2) × 2 × 25 - (1/2) × 2 × 36 = 189 J

questions.body: A person on the top of a tower observes a scooter moving with uniform velocity towards the base of the tower. He finds that the angle of depression changes from 30° to 60° in 18 minutes. The scooter will reach the base of the tower in next :

choices.d: the time depends upon the height of the tower

questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
Let the speed of the scooter = v m/min.
Distance BC = 18 v and Distance CD = t v.
In ΔACD
tan 60° = AD/CD
√3 = h/(t v)
h = √3 t v

questions.body: The ratio of amounts of H₂S needed to precipitate all the metal ions from 100 ml of 1 M AgNO₃ and 100 ml of 1 m CuSO₄ will be
questions.subject: chemistry
questions.solution:
2AgNO₃(aq.)+H₂S(g) → Ag₂S(s) + 2HNO₃(aq.)
CuSO₄ (aq.)+ H₂S (g) → CuS (s) + H₂SO₄(aq.)
According to the equations, ratio of the amounts of H₂S needed for precipitation is 1:2.

questions.body: P, Q and R are three natural numbers such that P and Q are primes and Q divides PR. Then out of the following the correct statement is :
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
P, Q → prime number
PR is divisible by Q. R is a multiple of Q.
Option A → Q divides R is correct.
Option B → P is not a multiple or R.
∴ wrong
Option C →
P is prime & R is not multiple of P
∴ wrong
Option D → P divides PQ is correct.

questions.body: A cylinder of radius 6 cm and height h cm is filled with ice cream. The ice cream is then distributed among 10 children in identical cones having hemispherical tops. The radius of the base of the cone is 3 cm and its height is 12cm. Then the height h of the cylinder must be :
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
Volume of icecream in cylinder
= volume of icecream in 12 cones
π(6)² × h = 10[1/3 π(3)² × 12 + 2/3 π(3)³]
36 h = 10 [36 + 18]
h = 15 cm.

questions.body: The number of isosceles triangles with integer sides such that no side is greater than 4 units is :
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
Isosceles triangles
Maximum side = 4 units
a < b + c
b < a + c
c < a + b
Possible combinations:
4, 4, 1    3, 3, 4    2, 2, 3
4, 4, 2    3, 3, 2    2, 2, 1
4, 4, 3    3, 3, 1    2, 2, 2
4, 4, 4    3, 3, 3    1, 1, 1
12 such combinations are possible.

questions.body: A man throws a ball making an angle of 60° with the horizontal. He runs on a level ground and catches the ball. If he had thrown the ball with speed v, then his speed must be :
questions.subject: physics
questions.solution:
R = v² 2sinθ cosθ/g
T = 2v sinθ/g
man cause the range with constant speed
v' = R/T
R = 2v² sin θcosθ/g
= v cos θₛ = v cos 60° = v/2
v' = v cosθ = vcos60 = v/2

questions.body: There are four resistors of 12 ohm each. Which of the following values is/are possible by their combinations (series and/or parallel)?
questions.subject: physics
questions.solution:
Circuit analysis with resistors:
(i) R = 12Ω in series
(ii) Parallel combination: 4Ω + 12Ω = 16Ω
(iii) Series-parallel combination: 5Ω + 6 = 30Ω
(iv) Complex resistor network = 9Ω

choices.c: It experiences equal up thrust from water and oil.

questions.subject: physics

questions.body: If p, q and r are positive real numbers, then the quantity (p + r)/(q + r) is
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
p, q, r are positive real numbers.
Difference = (p+r)/(q+r) - p/q
= (pq + rq - pq - rp)/(q(q+r)) = r(q-p)/(q(q+r))
p, q, r is positive.
Hence, the difference is positive, if q > p
and negative when p < q.
Hence, A & D is the solution.

questions.body: The expression (5a − 3b)³ + (3b − 7c)³ − (5a − 7c)³ is divisible by
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
(5a - 3b)³ + (3b - 7c)³ - (5a - 7c)³
Let 5a - 3b = x, 3b - 7c = y
and 7c - 5a = z
x³ + y³ + z³ = 3 xyz, if x + y + z = 0.
x + y + z = 5a - 3b + 3b - 7c + 7c - 5a = 0
(5a - 3b)³ + (3b - 7c)³ + (7c - 5a)³
= 3(5a - 3b)(3b - 7c)(7c - 5a)
∴ It is divisible by
5a - 3b, 3b - 7c, 7c - 5a

questions.body: In the figure shown:
questions.subject: maths
questions.skip_reason: Cannot solve without the geometric figure showing the angle relationships
questions.solution:
Circle with points A, B, C, D, O
∠ACB = (1/2)∠AOB
∠ACB = 70°
ABCD is cyclic quadrilateral
∴ ∠ADB + ∠ACB = 180°
∠ADB = 180° - 70° = 110°
So, θ = 110°
□AOBD
∠OAD + ∠O + ∠OBD + ∠D = 360°
α + 140° + β + 110° = 360°
∴ α + β = 110°
